summary: Schema migration mixes rename refactor with behavior change and leaves deprecated dead code.

findings.evidence: `resolveDisplay` mixes three intents in one method: V2 title-case formatting (`replaceFirstChar`), V1-to-V2 fallback routing (`findV2`/`findV1` branching), and the method's own signature change.
findings.minimal_fix: Split `resolveDisplay()` into two commits: first rename-only refactor preserving exact output, then a separate commit adding V2 `replaceFirstChar` title-case formatting.
findings.verification: Run `DisplayNameService` tests after the rename-only commit and assert identical outputs before proceeding to the behavior commit.

findings.evidence: `UserRepository.save()` writes to both `v1Store` and `v2Store`, while `findV1`/`findV2` expose both schemas with no migration phase boundary.
findings.minimal_fix: Define explicit migration phases: (1) dual-write window with parity tests, (2) V2-read cutover, (3) V1-write removal with a deletion PR checklist.
findings.verification: Add parity tests asserting `findV1()` and `findV2()` return equivalent display names for all saved users before entering the cutover phase.

findings.evidence: `getDisplayName` is @Deprecated with no callers, but remains in the codebase indefinitely.
findings.minimal_fix: Remove `getDisplayName()` after confirming zero call sites via a grep or IDE find-usages check.
findings.verification: Run a full project build and verify no compilation errors or unresolved references after `getDisplayName()` deletion.

findings.evidence: No tests verify display-name parity between V1 fallback and V2 paths during the migration window.
findings.minimal_fix: Add parameterized tests for `DisplayNameService` with V2-only, V1-only, and mixed-store scenarios asserting identical display names.
findings.verification: Execute the `DisplayNameService` test suite and confirm both code paths produce equivalent outputs for matching user data.
